calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jun-08-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michelle McGill for and with Naomi Allen. Sokolsky Lacey in Propria Persona.

Ms. McGill stated that she received the bank statements this morning and that she will file them. Mr. Lacey raised the issue of conflict of interest with Ms. McGill representing Ms. Allen stating that he paid her $1,500.00 to retain her, concern about the Decedent's personal belongings, his vehicle, his Mother's vehicle and his personal property. Ms. McGill stated that Mr. Lacey will get the $1,500.00 back, that the Mother's personal property were in possession of Mr. Sheppard and not in this Estate, that the Administrator has personal photos for Mr. Lacey and that his vehicle was left on the premises when Mr. Lacey vacated the premises and were considered abandoned. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar May-16-2012 Continued To Jun-05-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Off Calendar Dec-06-2011 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Approval Of Eviction Attorney Feees And Reimbursement Of Property Expenses From Sale Proceeds; Declaration Of Naomi Allen, Executor; Declaration Of Jerod Hendrickson, Esquire
Off Calendar Dec-06-2011 Continued To Dec-27-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Edward Otonello and Michele McGill for and with Naomi Allen (Executor).

The sale was confirmed at $400,000.00 subject to the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the petition (subject to review of the order). The proceeds from the sale are to be deposited into a blocked account. Status of filing of the receipt for the blocked account was set for December 27, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Petition for Approval of Eviction Attorney Fees, etc. was continued to December 27, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for re-notice/proper notice.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Nov-16-2010 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Nov-02-2010 Continued To Nov-16-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Oct-12-2010 Continued To Nov-02-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Sep-07-2010 Continued To Oct-12-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michelle McGill for Naomi Allen (Executor). Mary Jane Gordon for and with Sheppard Harris.

Ms. Gordon stated that Mr. Harris filed his petition because he was not aware that his Wife had an estate plan, that the Golden One account was in Esterlene's name with a balance of $17,446.98, that the funds were withdrawn by Ms. Lacey's Son Eugene and that Mr. Harris had no access to that account. In addition, Ms. Gordon stated that the Citibank account was a loan in the amount of $17,500.00 that had been paid off by Ms. Lacey and that there was no money in that account, that they only had a small joint account with Wells Fargo where the Wife's social security went, an account with Bank of America where his pension went, that they were married for 11 years and that they lived together for three years prior to the marriage. Ms. Gordon also stated that the car was purchased in 1999, that it was inoperable for some time and that Mr. Harris recently repaired the car, that they only inventoried the house as it was the only significant asset and that the rest was community property. The matter was continued to October 12, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. McGill is to advise the Court as to whether there are any further objections by October 5, 2010 so that the matter could be taken under submission without an appearance on the October 12th hearing date.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jan-05-2010 Continued To Jan-19-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sokolski Eugene Lacey (Son) in Propria Persona.

Mr. Lacey appeared to object to the petition, stated that his Grandmother left the house to his Mother and expressed concern about Mr. Harris having control of all of the Decedent's belongings. The matter was continued to January 19, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court suggested Mr. Lacey obtain Counsel.
